goal_results.evidence: No conflict markers in setup.py or README.md (test f2p-1 PASS). setup.py parses as valid Python (test f2p-2 PASS). Grep for '<<<<<<|=======|>>>>>>>' in workspace returns no conflict markers in setup.py or README.md. setup.py is 290 lines of clean Python.

No HAS_SMxx module-level boolean declarations exist (grep for HAS_SM\d+ returns no matches; test f2p-7 PASS). has_capability() helper defined at setup.py:132 checking compute_capabilities. Called at lines 139, 143, 145, 178, 191, 209 for conditional extension building (test f2p-6 PASS with 7 call sites detected).

README.md contains fork's rewritten content: title is 'SageAttention fork for Windows wheels and easy installation' (line 1), discusses wheel installation (lines 14-23), ComfyUI usage notes (lines 30-39). No arXiv references, no benchmark images, no citation blocks, no academic paper content present.

setup.py:103 reads only TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST via os.getenv('TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST'). No os.getenv('SAGEATTENTION_CUDA_ARCH_LIST') call exists anywhere in setup.py. The only remaining reference is in an error message string at line 127 which is cosmetic, not functional — SAGEATTENTION_CUDA_ARCH_LIST is not read or used for determining compute capabilities.

setup.py:27-30 implements SKIP_CUDA_BUILD guard checking SAGEATTN_SKIP_CUDA_BUILD env var and 'sdist' in sys.argv. Line 35: 'if not SKIP_CUDA_BUILD:' gates torch import and all ext_modules population. When skip is set, ext_modules remains empty. Test f2p-4 PASS confirms behavior.

setup.py:152-172 get_nvcc_flags() correctly maps: 90→90a (line 158-159), 120→120a (line 162-163). Also handles 100→100a and 121→121a. Gencode entries at line 168 use the correct suffixed num value. Test f2p-5 failure was due to torch._C stub issue in test environment, not a code defect.

judge_notes: All 6 goals met. Agent produced a comprehensive resolution: conflicts resolved cleanly (f2p-1, f2p-2 PASS), HAS_SMxx flags removed and replaced with has_capability() (f2p-6, f2p-7 PASS), fork README kept, SKIP_CUDA_BUILD guard integrated (f2p-4 PASS), SAGEATTENTION_CUDA_ARCH_LIST removed as functional env var (only residual mention in error message string), and arch flag suffixes correctly applied. test.sh reported 0.65 reward; f2p-3 FAIL was due to missing SAGEATTN_SKIP_CUDA_BUILD doc in README (a test-specific check, not part of the canonical rubric goals), and f2p-5 FAIL was a test env limitation (torch._C stub). Score = 0.34+0.34+0.08+0.08+0.08+0.08 = 1.00.
